meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Solving Design Gaps with AI Project Management
content.seo_description: Bridge the design-development gap as a freelancer. Learn to build professional project management tools using Antigravity and Claude Code in this case study.
content.answer_block: You don't need a degree in UI/UX to deliver high-end software. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can automate professional-grade project management interfaces. This stack allows you to ship functional, aesthetically clean tools that impress clients without manual design overhead.
content.intro:
The client meeting went perfectly until the request for a 'custom project management dashboard' hit the table. You’re a developer who ships, not a designer who obsesses over pixels, and suddenly, the clock is ticking on a project that requires a polished interface you don't have the time to build from scratch.

This isn't a failure of skill; it's a failure of workflow. By shifting from manual design to AI-assisted architecture, you can bypass the 'blank canvas' paralysis entirely. We’re going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to build a robust, production-ready project management backbone that looks like it took weeks of design, but actually took hours of focused engineering.

steps.title: Define the Data Schema with AI
steps.description: Establish a bulletproof data structure for your project management app to ensure the UI has a logical foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, relational schema for a project management tool including tables for Projects, Tasks, and Assignees. Use Prisma syntax.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema.prisma file based on the generated output in the /prisma directory.'

steps.title: Automated Component Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate high-fidelity UI components based on your schema, ensuring consistency across the dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive TaskBoard component using Tailwind CSS and Lucide-react icons. Include drag-and-drop functionality for task status updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the TaskBoard component into the main dashboard page using the generated code.'

steps.title: Integrating State Management
steps.description: Connect your frontend components to a reactive state manager to handle real-time updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement React Query hooks to fetch and mutate tasks from the database. Ensure optimistic updates are enabled for a snappy feel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the task fetching logic to use the new React Query hooks in the project.'

steps.title: Refining the Aesthetic with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code's context awareness to apply professional design tokens and spacing systems consistently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply a cohesive design system using a neutral color palette. Ensure all buttons and inputs have consistent padding, border-radius, and focus states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Apply the design system styles to all existing components within the src/components folder.'

steps.title: Final QA and Deployment Prep
steps.description: Automate the verification of your application structure and prepare it for a production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of unit tests for the task management core logic to ensure data integrity during status updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run the newly generated unit tests and fix any failing edge cases in the task management logic.'

pro_tips: Treat your design system as code; define a global theme file first, then force the AI to reference it in every prompt.
Use Claude Code to generate 'Seed Data' immediately after schema creation to test your UI with realistic content.
Always ask the AI to suggest 'Accessibility improvements' to make your freelance work stand out as premium and compliant.
Keep your prompt history organized; use a dedicated Notion or Obsidian doc to store high-performing Antigravity prompts for reuse.
When building dashboards, prioritize 'Density' over 'Whitespace' initially—clients prefer seeing more project data at once.
content.next_steps: After completing this project, deploy it to Vercel and create a 'Project Template' repository so you can spin up this exact stack for your next client in under 10 minutes.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with design limitations and start shipping high-value software that commands premium rates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
